focus: REQ-008 async-ingest fromScan fix verification + same-class producer sweep

claim: Both arms of the processFile ternary in the initIngestJobQueue callback pass fromScan: true: the governance-truthy arm emits {governance, fromScan: true}, and the governance-falsy arm emits {provenance: {provenanceSource: 'memory_ingest_start', provenanceActor: 'async-ingest', tool: 'memory_ingest_start'}, fromScan: true}. The conditional has no alternate branch, no early return, and no helper indirection — every execution path through the callback carries fromScan: true.
counterevidence: none — body is exactly await indexSingleFile(filePath, false, governance ? {...} : {...}) with no other emission site.
alternativeExplanation: a developer could refactor the conditional into separate calls or split into a default-param helper — current code does neither, and T47c-2 pins both arms' source shape.
downgradeTrigger: if either arm's fromScan: true were dropped, T47c-2 fails on the source side; a runtime miss requires bypassing indexSingleFile entirely.

claim: Independent rg -n 'fromScan' over mcp-server/ (excluding dist/) returns exactly four producer-side emits (startupScan, processFile governance arm, processFile provenance arm, file-watcher reindexFn, handleMemoryIndexScan worker loop). The remaining hits are the indexSingleFile signature/body forwarder and the consumer-side resolveIndexingOrigin — not producers. No fifth producer was missed.
counterevidence: none — grep over indexMemoryFile( returns the same four producers plus its own definition; no other call site exists.
alternativeExplanation: a future caller could invoke indexSingleFile(filePath, false, {}) omitting fromScan — current code contains no such caller.
downgradeTrigger: any future indexSingleFile/indexMemoryFile caller that omits fromScan would be a new REQ-008 violation; the source-pattern test does not catch this because it only matches the specific processFile block, not all call sites.

claim: Crash-recovery replay path inherits the REQ-008 fix without its own re-entry point. initIngestJobQueue (739-751) calls resetIncompleteJobsToQueued (227-250, which only UPDATEs DB rows) and loops enqueueIngestJob (700-733) for each reset ID; enqueueIngestJob pushes into pendingQueue and starts drainQueue (668-698); drainQueue invokes processQueuedJob (584-664) which at 635 calls await processFileFn(nextPath, current.governance). processFileFn is the module-scoped variable declared at 117 and assigned once at 740 from config.processFile — the same callback registered at boot (context-server.ts:2443).
counterevidence: none — processFileFn is referenced only at 117 (declare), 585-587 (guard), 635 (invocation), 740 (init assignment); no setter or re-init function is exported.
alternativeExplanation: a future refactor splitting initIngestJobQueue into register-callback and start-worker phases could allow a second registration — current code's single-assignment pattern prevents this.
downgradeTrigger: if processFileFn were reassigned or if a second worker entry point were added without fromScan: true, the crash-recovery guarantee would break silently because the source-pattern test only pins the boot-time callback shape, not the replay path's identity with it.

claim: T47c and T47c-2 are purely source-pattern regex assertions over a captured sourceCode string. T47c regex-matches the processFile block shape (callback signature + await indexSingleFile call inside); T47c-2 extracts the processFile block and asserts the governance ternary emits {governance, fromScan: true} and the provenance branch emits fromScan: true immediately after the provenance object. Neither test invokes the callback at runtime, mocks indexSingleFile, or asserts any DB/filesystem side-effect. This mirrors the established T47d convention (REQ-006) at lines 2583-2586. The runtime correctness of the fromScan-gated persistQualityLoopContent branch is covered by the two real-DB tests in handler-memory-index.vitest.ts (referenced at implementation-summary.md:73, 119).
counterevidence: none — direct read of lines 2568-2581 shows only expect(...).toMatch(...) calls and a captured sourceCode variable; no await processFile(...) invocation in either test body.
alternativeExplanation: the structural asymmetry is between the GATE (covered at runtime in handler-memory-index.vitest.ts) and the CALLER (covered by source-pattern in context-server.vitest.ts) — a deliberate convention that pins caller shape but cannot enumerate every other indexSingleFile call site that omits fromScan.
downgradeTrigger: if a future indexSingleFile caller were added outside the processFile block without fromScan: true, T47c-2 would not catch it; recommend documenting this convention in checklist.md alongside the existing chunked-branch coverage caveat at implementation-summary.md:155-157.

evidence: implementation-summary.md:193 compared to context-server.ts:2442-2456 (processFile callback both branches carry fromScan: true), :1729 and :2488 (same-class producers confirmed), lib/ops/job-queue.ts:739-751 + :635 (crash-replay path goes through same processFileFn callback).

T47c-2 (context-server.vitest.ts:2575-2581) is the cited shape-evidence test; CHK for REQ-008 in checklist.md not yet located in this iteration — not blocking since implementation-summary.md:193,236 documents the evidence chain end-to-end and the implementer explicitly notes the convention consistency with REQ-006's T47d (implementation-summary.md:122).
no skill-authoring change in REQ-008 scope
no agent-definition change in REQ-008 scope
no playbook change in REQ-008 scope

nextDimension: REQ-009 background-scan default at MCP tool dispatch boundary — verify lifecycle-tools.ts and tool-schemas.ts, and independently confirm the two claimed internal-caller exemptions (CLI reindex in cli.ts/launcher-ipc-bridge.cjs, boot-time drift repair in context-server.ts) actually bypass the new default correctly.
